extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won stage 7 in a photo finish by millimeters over BOASSON HAGEN Edvald after losing position 150m out but timing his bike throw perfectly in final half-meter
tactical_patterns: KITTEL starts sprint from third wheel after SABATINI Fabio drop-off, closer positioning than previous wins allows conservation for final acceleration
JANSE VAN RENSBURG Reinardt pulls off 180-200m out on right bend forcing BOASSON HAGEN inside line, opening door for KITTEL right-side pass
Wind dying at 5pm prevented expected GC splits despite dangerous finale turns, SKY kept FROOME front but crosswinds threat never materialized
BOASSON HAGEN throws bike microseconds late with poor pedal stroke timing, showing non-specialist sprint positioning disadvantage versus KITTEL professionalism

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: recovered from being half-bike length behind at 50m through superior throw timing, third win shows peak sprint form
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: positive: unexpectedly competitive sprint finish suggests form renaissance, but throw timing deficiency versus pure sprinters exposed
rider_signals.JANSE VAN RENSBURG Reinardt: positive: elite lead-out execution placing BOASSON HAGEN perfectly 180m out, wasted without Cavendish behind
rider_signals.SABATINI Fabio: positive: KITTEL third-wheel drop-off at optimal distance shows Quick-Step level lead-out train now functional
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Team SKY prudently maintained front position through technical finale despite wind threat diminishing
